stories.title: US urges contractor to evacuate workers from Kuwait and Iraq over worries of Iran-backed attacks
stories.summary: After Guardian reports about danger to V2X employees, sources say state department raised concerns with defense contractor Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US government has called on the defense contractor V2X to evacuate its employees from Kuwait and Iraq , warning the company that they could be targeted by Iran -backed militias, four sources said. The intervention follows reporting by the Guardian that V2X employees were stationed at US militar

stories.title: Von der Leyen, Costa congratulate Russia-aligned Radev on Bulgaria election win
stories.summary: Top European officials on Monday congratulated Bulgaria’s next leader Rumen Radev after his Progressive Bulgaria party won Sunday’s election, despite some concerns over his Russia-sympathetic positions. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said she was looking forward to working with Radev “for the prosperity and security” of Europe. “Bulgaria is a proud member of the European family and plays an important role in tackling our common challenges,” she wrote on X . European Cou

stories.title: New Hungarian leader Magyar tells president and other Orbán appointees to step down by end of May – Europe live
stories.summary: Key judicial leaders among those told to go by the end of next month or face removal by the new administration The commission also got asked about the Italian proposals for a “wild west-style bounties” that could be paid to Italian lawyers if they successfully convince their immigrant clients to return home. Our Rome correspondent Angela Giuffrida reported on the controversial proposal over the weekend: Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Europe needs homegrown missile defence in a year – Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Ukrainian president discusses Patriot alternative with other countries; Bulgarian election may be new headache for Kyiv. What we know on day 1,517 Europe must have its own defence system against ballistic missiles and Ukraine is holding talks with several countries to create one, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Sunday. Ukraine relies heavily on scant supplies of the Patriot system, produced by the US, to shoot down Russian missiles, which are often fired at Ukraine’s electricity generation and trans

stories.title: Le Pen, Bardella offer to slash red tape in overture to business leaders
stories.summary: PARIS — Far-right leaders Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella pledged on Monday to “liberate” the French economy by slashing red tape and bureaucracy. “Regulations, often accumulated without real assessment of their impact, represent a staggering cost for economic actors … [and] decrease the purchasing power of French citizens,” they wrote in a joint letter to business leaders published on X.  According to the text, Le Pen and Bardella are tasking a group of

stories.title: Europe’s last coal – a photo essay
stories.summary: In Poland, 80,000 people still work in coalmines – the last in the European Union that is fully committed to the energy transition. Once active mines are being converted to other uses, and yet coal is being extracted at record rates worldwide, and with the Iran war pushing up oil and gas prices, some in Poland are asking whether it is worth completely phasing out this fossil fuel Coal dust is fine; it seeps into the pores of the skin. That is why a thin black line permanently traces the outline

stories.title: Slovenia’s prime minister admits he cannot form a government
stories.summary: Slovenia’s caretaker prime minister, Robert Golob , on Monday admitted his party was unable to form a government, potentially paving the way for the return of populist Janez Janša. Golob’s liberal Freedom Movement won last month’s national election by a razor-thin margin , securing 29 of the 90 seats in the Slovenian parliament, but failed to secure a governing majority. After a month of failed coalition talks, Golob said he had informed Slovenian President Nataša Pirc Musar th

stories.title: Co Lee és a 33 dalos Air 2: új korszak indul a Budapest Parkban (x)
stories.summary: Április 20-án debütál Co Lee és don sea közös nagylemeze, az Air 2, amely összesen 26 trackkel és egy különleges vinyl-kiadáson 7 bónuszdallal feszegeti a hazai hiphop határait. A WAVY Records gondozásában megjelent anyag alkotói folyamata egyfajta belső meditációként, lassan hömpölygő folyóként érkezik a hallgatókhoz. A valaha volt legnagyobb volumenű magyar nyelvű lo-fi hiphop anyag négyévnyi munka gyümölcseként ölt testet.

stories.title: Hiányolja Orbán lemondását Király Nóra, a Fidesz kikapott csepeli jelöltje
stories.summary: Király Nóra szerint már a választás estéjén le kellett volna mondania a Fidesz elnökének.

stories.title: Forsthoffer Ágnes: Legyen a parlamenti munka, a közélet és a közbeszéd példamutató
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt alelnöke megtisztelőnek tartja, hogy Magyar Péter őt kérte fel az Országgyűlés elnöki pozíciójára.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: 16 minisztérium lesz a Tisza-kormányban
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök hét miniszterét nevezi meg a Tisza Párt frakcióülése után.

stories.title: Nincs NMP-szennyezés az akkumulátorgyárak környékén a Greenpeace szerint, de ez nem teszi semmissé a korábbi, határértéket túllépő méréseket
stories.summary: A Greenpeace lakossági kérésre vizsgálódott 10 helyszínen, ezúttal nem találtak határérték feletti koncentrációt sem NMP-ben, sem a mért fémekben.

stories.title: Miért nem akarunk néha szembenézni azzal, hogy szabályt szegünk?
stories.summary: Miért nem járunk utána a tényeknek? Miért nem akarunk sokszor a dolgok mögé nézni, és szeretnénk néha tudatlanok maradni? A Telex podcast stúdiójában Dr. Szvetelszky Zsuzsa szociálpszichológus és Hajdu Márton, a Philip Morris Magyarország Kft. szóvivője volt a vendégünk.

stories.title: Gruevszki és a lengyel VIP-menekültek orbáni védelme hamar semmivé lesz
stories.summary: „Magyarország nem nemzetközileg körözött bűnözők lerakóhelye” – üzente meg Magyar Péter. A politikai menekültstátusz gyorsan megvonható, de a volt lengyel igazságügyi miniszter és helyettese közül csak utóbbi ellen van nemzetközi elfogatóparancs is érvényben. A volt észak-macedón kormányfő esetét bonyolítja, hogy hazája jelenlegi vezetése nem kér belőle.

stories.title: The best of Magyar vs Orbán
stories.summary: For two years, Fidesz struggled to get a handle on Péter Magyar. No matter what they tried, they came up empty-handed. Our video looks back at the steps that led to the Tisza Party’s unprecedented victory.

stories.title: Filmtörténeti esemény: májusban jön az Eurocine, a filmipari kiállítás (x)
stories.summary: Az elmúlt évek egyik legizgalmasabb vállalkozása kezdődik május 8-án a Hungexpo Budapest Kongresszusi és Kiállítási Központban. Budapest ad otthont ugyanis az Eurocine 2026 nemzetközi filmipari szakkiállításnak. A nemzetközi hírű brit SCS Concepts and Exhibitions Ltd. és az immár saját filmes üzletágat is működtető HUNGEXPO együttműködésében megvalósuló kétnapos esemény Magyarországon és a teljes régióban is hiánypótló; ehhez fogható platformon még nem találkozhatott a mozgóképes szakma Kelet-Kö

stories.title: Eurostat: 2100-ig 22,5 százalékos lesz a népességfogyás Magyarországon
stories.summary: A demográfiai előrejelzések szerint 53 millió fővel csökken Európa lakossága a század végéig.

stories.title: The Kremlin got over Orbán in no time
stories.summary: Orbán, who adopted a subservient position towards the Russians and engaged in confrontation with the EU, was useful to Moscow. As the new government does not wish to sever all economic ties, the Russian leadership quickly came to view the Tisza cabinet as a suitable partner for pragmatic relations. They, however, chose to ignore Magyar’s statements on the war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Újra fellép Budapesten Morrissey, a The Smiths egykori frontembere
stories.summary: A nagy koncertlemondó hírében álló előadónak ez lesz a harmadik fellépése nálunk, korábban a Pecsában és a Millenárison zenélt.

stories.title: Több mint 4 milliárd forintot utalt a Bethlen Gábor Alapkezelő vajdasági szervezeteknek a választások előtt
stories.summary: Szinte az összes szervezet jó kapcsolatot ápol a Vajdasági Magyar Szövetséggel, aminek elnöke együtt kampányolt Orbán Viktorral.

stories.title: Vizsgálja az adatvédelmi hatóság, hogy használták-e Magyarországon a Webloc nevű megfigyelőszoftvert
stories.summary: A VSquare írt arról, hogy a magyar állam szervei már 2022 óta használhatják a programot.

stories.title: A Független Benzinkutak Szövetsége szerint az Orbán-kormány nem tartotta be az ígéretét, bezáró kutakra figyelmeztetnek
stories.summary: A benzinkutak működtetése súlyos veszteséget okoz, az állami támogatás pedig késik.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Egyhangúlag támogatta a Tisza-frakció Bujdosó Andrea és Forsthoffer Ágnes jelölését
stories.summary: Még tart a Tisza frakcióülése.

stories.title: Tizenhét gimnázium kéri Magyar Pétert, vegye figyelembe a szakmai álláspontot az oktatási miniszter kiválasztásánál
stories.summary: A levelet aláírók között van többek között a budapesti Fazekas és az Eötvös József Gimnázium igazgatója is.

stories.title: Orbán's protection of Gruevski and Polish VIP fugitives may soon vanish into thin air
stories.summary: “Hungary is not a dumping ground for internationally wanted criminals,” Péter Magyar said. Although revoking political asylum can be done relatively quickly, things are not as simple as they may seem at first glance. A look at what Marcinowsky, Ziobro and Gruevski may expect after Orbán's departure from power.

stories.title: Magyar Péter bejelentett hét minisztert, és május 31-ig adott időt a NER által kinevezett hivatalvezetőknek a távozásra
stories.summary: Nem lesznek csúcsminisztériumok, minden szakterületnek külön minisztere lesz, jelentette be a kormányfői poszt várományosa. Nagyon sok szakértőt megkérdeztek az oktatási miniszter személyéjéről, de szívesen fogadják mások a javaslatait is. A Tisza kampányfőnöke szerint beigazolódott, hogy az országjárásoknak volt ereje.

stories.title: Alig bírt moccanni az oroszlánmajmocska a hangyász ölelésétől a budapesti állatkertben
stories.summary: A kis majomnak barátra volt szüksége, kettőt is talált.

stories.title: Itt vannak a leendő Magyar-kormány minisztériumai
stories.summary: Tizenhat miniszter lesz Magyar Péter kormányában, hét nevet már most elárult.

stories.title: Orbán Anita, a leendő külügyminiszter lehorgonyozna a nyugati szövetségi rendszerben, és felszámolná az orosz függőséget
stories.summary: Orbán Anita a 2010-es évek első felében még Orbán Viktor kormányában szolgált diplomataként, de atlantistaként és az orosz energiafüggőség ellenzőjeként az évtized közepére kiszorult onnan. Első feladatai egyikének a Beneš-dekrétumok ügyének rendezését tekinti.

stories.title: More than half of Hungarians agree with Magyar's call for resignations of public dignitaries, according to survey
stories.summary: The prime-minister elect said they need to go because they were all Fidesz-appointees, and the regime change wouldn't be complete without their departure.

stories.title: Zelenszkij a pénzszállítókról: Ezt a pénzt Orbán ellopta, de erről már Magyarral fogunk tudni beszélni
stories.summary: Az ukrán elnök ígéretet tett, hogy a Barátság kőolajvezeték április végéig újra használható lesz. Az új magyar kormány felállása Zelenszkij szerint lehetőség mindkét országnak és az EU egészének is a kérdések rendezésére.

stories.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
stories.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

stories.title: Péter Magyar’s real coup was winning over loyal Orbán voters – not preaching to the converted
stories.summary: He is no progressive but unlike the old opposition, the Tisza leader listened to Orbán’s rural base The international audience observing the Hungarian election result is likely to settle on a view that feels familiar. That this election was about east v west or that it was a “youthquake”, a win secured by the unprecedented participation of young voters. These narratives have some truth to them, of course, but, especially for those interested in fighting back against regimes such as Viktor Orbán’

stories.title: Hormuz standoff shows no signs of letting up for vital shipping to resume from Gulf
stories.summary: Maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz remained at minimal levels on Friday resulting in a giant parking lot, as the US-Iran standoff continues with the same cautious approach since Monday.

stories.title: Arrests fuel fears among Madagascar’s gen Z protesters that new regime no better than one they overthrew
stories.summary: Jubilation is turning to disenchantment as young activists arrested after protest calling for election date to be set The arrest of several protesters in Madagascar has increased fears among young people that the military regime that took power last year after huge Gen Z demonstrations will be no better than the government it overthrew. stories.title: Minns doubles down on ‘rational’ anti-protest law despite NSW’s highest court ruling it unconstitutional
stories.summary: Greens criticise premier’s ‘extraordinary attack’ on judiciary and urge him to accept he ‘got it wrong’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales premier has doubled down on an anti-protest law struck down in the state’s highest court last week, defending the legislation introduced by his government as “rational and proportionate”. But advocates for protesters charged at demonstrations restricted under the laws have criticised Chris Minns’ comments, calling

stories.title: French conservative leader Retailleau gets party backing for presidential bid
stories.summary: PARIS — French conservative leader Bruno Retailleau has been endorsed by members of his party, Les Républicains, to run in next year’s already crowded race to succeed President Emmanuel Macron. Retailleau, who had already declared his intention to run in the presidential election, won his party’s official backing after members were internally surveyed to determine whether they preferred to hold a primary to select a candidate, or back Les Républicains’ leader outright. More tha

stories.title: 5 times Keir Starmer blamed the deep state
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer was meant to be the bureaucrats’ prime minister. Now he’s dumping on them. Under fire over multiple missteps since taking office in 2024, Starmer, the former chief prosecutor for England and Wales, has been blaming his top officials. He has already parted company with a cabinet secretary, and is now having a bitter public spat with the ex-Foreign Office chief whom he ousted last Thursday over revelations that Peter Mandelson failed security vetting. stories.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
stories.summary: Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists. The south-east Asian country has been locked in civil war since 2021 , when the military usurped the democratic government and launched a violent crackdown on dissidents. stories.summary: Iran decried the seizure as an act of piracy, saying it has no plans to participate in talks with the US. Plus, how Maui residents are rebuilding Lahaina for locals, not tourists Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. The US military has attacked and seized an Iranian-flagged container ship that tried to evade its blockade near the strait of Hormuz. stories.summary: IDF says sledgehammer photo circulating online is authentic and is being viewed with ‘great severity’ The Israeli army has said it is investigating a soldier who was seen apparently striking a statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon. stories.summary: Non-appearance of Pam Bondi and remarks from Todd Blanche suggest full accounting may never be revealed In the days since Pam Bondi ’s exit from Donald Trump ’s justice department, Jeffrey Epstein survivors and transparency advocates have been confronted by mixed messaging, prompting questions about whether a full accounting of his crimes would ever be revealed. Legal veterans told the Guardian that authorities’ decisions – such as Bondi’s failure to appear for a congressional subpoena about her

stories.title: No 10 signals Starmer accepts he inadvertently misled parliament over Mandelson vetting – UK politics live
stories.summary: Downing Street spokesperson says PM ‘would never knowingly mislead parliament or the public’ Judgment day for PM over Mandelson scandal At his press conference Nigel Farage was asked about reports saying that Keir Starmer knew about the security concerns about Peter Mandelson that led to him failing his security vetting interview. stories.title: Iranian American woman arrested in Los Angeles for alleged arms trafficking
stories.summary: Federal prosecutor says woman is suspected of dealing weapons to Africa on behalf of Iranian government A California woman was arrested at Los Angeles international airport after allegedly trafficking weapons on behalf of the Iranian government to contacts in Africa, including Sudan. Shamim Mafi, 44, of Woodland Hills was detained on Saturday night by federal agents, according to the top federal prosecutor in Los Angeles. Continue reading...

stories.title: Will Venezuela's oil sector reform attract investors?
stories.summary: After the US ousted Maduro and effectively seized control of Venezuela's oil industry, the country's economy has been transitioning rapidly. The interim government is wooing investors but lacks democratic backing.

stories.title: US strike on alleged drug boat in Caribbean kills 3
stories.summary: Trump has said that the US is in "armed conflict" with cartels, as its military campaign has killed at least 180 people since last year amid criticism over its legality.

stories.title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills at least three people in the Caribbean Sea
stories.summary: US strikes on drug boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean have so far killed a minimum of 181 people. Critics have slammed the Trump administration over the strikes, saying they constitute a violation of international law.

stories.title: WWII bomb destroyed in controlled explosion in Paris
stories.summary: Video provided by French police shows the controlled detonation of a large bomb dating from the Second World War in Colombes. After attempts to remove the bomb’s detonator failed at midday, authorities buried it and later destroyed it in a controlled detonation.

stories.title: Iranian woman arrested in US for allegedly trafficking arms to Sudan
stories.summary: Shamim Mafi is accused of brokering the sales of arms to Sudan's defence ministry on behalf of Iran.

stories.title: French budget faces €4B in cuts as Iran war hampers growth
stories.summary: PARIS ― France is looking at slashing at least €4 billion in spending this year to keep its public finances under control. With France’s economic outlook for the year deteriorating as a result of the war in the Middle East, the government is looking for ways to make sure its budget deficit — the gap between what it collects in taxes and what it spends — doesn’t exceed 5 percent of gross domestic product, as Paris envisioned when it passed its 2026 budget. stories.title: Fire destroys 1,000 ‘stilt’ homes in Malaysia’s Sabah, displacing thousands
stories.summary: Blaze struck a ‘water village’ that is home to some of Malaysia’s poorest residents A huge fire destroyed about 1,000 makeshift homes, many of them built on stilts over water, and displaced thousands of people in a coastal village in Malaysia’s Sabah state on Sunday, authorities said. stories.title: Weather tracker: temperatures in Spain and Brazil well above late April norm
stories.summary: Seville could see 34C this week and parts of Brazil could hit high 30s, while storms forecast in southern Africa Over the course of this week, temperatures in Spain are expected to soar well above the seasonal average. Daytime temperatures could reach about 30C in Madrid on Tuesday, 10C above the norm, while Seville may experience 34C, about 9C above its late April average. stories.title: Moscow-friendly Rumen Radev wins absolute majority in Bulgarian elections
stories.summary: Former president’s election could end years of weak coalition governments but critics warn it may be bad for EU Bulgaria’s Moscow-friendly former president has won an absolute majority in parliamentary elections that could bring the country political stability after years of short-lived coalitions, but leave it walking a tightrope on EU issues. stories.title: Do You Come from Gomorrah? review – Frank McGuinness’s blistering portrait of abuse and prejudice
stories.summary: Abbey, Dublin An unnamed narrator recollects a 1970s childhood of institutional brutality and sectarianism in this allusive memory play Language is twisted and slippery in Frank McGuinness disturbing new memory play for the Abbey theatre. As an unnamed narrator, Man (Ryan Donaldson) looks back on his 1970s youth during the Troubles in Northern Ireland, he says that the past “does not belong to me”. stories.title: Electric car sales soar 51% in mainland Europe as Iran war drives up fuel prices
stories.summary: Data shows 224,000 new EVs were registered in March, with Norway leading way in terms of switching Business live – latest updates Sales of electric cars soared 51% in continental Europe last month, amid a rise in petrol and diesel costs driven by the Iran war. stories.title: Amy Winehouse’s father Mitch loses high court battle against her friends
stories.summary: Judge throws out claim by the singer’s father over the sale of items she once owned Amy Winehouse’s father has lost a high court claim against two of his daughter’s friends over the auctioning of items once owned by the singer. Mitch Winehouse, acting as the administrator of his daughter’s estate, sued her stylist Naomi Parry and friend Catriona Gourlay over claims they profited from selling dozens of items at auctions in the US in 2021 and 2023. stories.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments

stories.title: Starmer to admit he inadvertently gave MPs misleading information on Mandelson

In a post on X, US Central Command said US Marines had departed the amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli by helicopter and rappelled onto the Iranian-

stories.title: Canada's US ties have become a weakness, says Carney
stories.summary: Canadian PM Mark Carney has vowed to reduce his country's reliance on the US by strengthening the economy and inking trade deals with others.

stories.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
stories.summary: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

stories.title: Germany news: Timmy the stranded whale back on the move

stories.title: Japan issues tsunami alert after strong earthquake
stories.summary: The 7.4-magnitude quake off northern Iwate prefecture could be felt as far as Tokyo. Residents have been urged to evacuate northern coastal regions ahead of a tsunami.

stories.title: How China is reshaping the global chip industry

stories.summary: Amid stalled US-Iran negotiations, Iranian officials warning the crucial shipping lane will remain shut until the US lifts its blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: North Korea conducts multiple ballistic missile tests
stories.summary: North Korea fired several short-range ballistic missiles into the East Sea on Sunday, the latest in a series of recent weapons tests that analysts say signal Pyongyang's rejection of South Korea's attempts to ease tensions.

stories.title: Trump signs order to fast-track research on psychedelic drugs
stories.summary: Trump signed an executive order Saturday to ease research restrictions on psychedelic drugs like psilocybin and LSD, aiming to accelerate studies into their potential for treating mental health conditions.

stories.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
stories.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

stories.title: China hosts humanoid robots in Beijing half marathon
stories.summary: China has tested humanoid robots in a real race setting in Beijing. The event highlights advances in endurance, balance and AI behaviour. Engineers see it as a step towards wider use.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz standoff reignites as IRGC defies Iran’s diplomats
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz has been blocked again by Iran, as the IRGC’s hardline stance appears to prevail in Tehran. US Vice President JD Vance will lead the US delegation for talks in Pakistan on Monday, after President Trump said he would not attend.

stories.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
stories.summary: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

stories.title: Venezuela’s Machado to hold Madrid rally as opposition frozen out after Maduro capture
stories.summary: Exiled leader to revive push for change amid US backing of Delcy Rodríguez and delays to democratic transition Venezuela’s opposition leader, María Corina Machado, will seek to revive her push for political change with a rally in Madrid on Saturday, having found herself sidelined by Donald Trump after the abduction of the president Nicolás Maduro. “Venezuela will be free,” the Nobel peace prize winner insisted in an interview on the eve of this weekend’s demonstration in the Puerta del Sol squar

stories.title: Rome and Caucasus poised to further discuss closer energy ties
stories.summary: The Italian prime minister is expected to visit Azerbaijan in the coming weeks to continue political dialogue and energy cooperation between Rome and Baku, with Azerbaijani gas supplies via the Trans Adriatic Pipeline remaining a key pillar of bilateral ties.

stories.title: Kenyan firm sacks more than 1,000 workers after losing Meta contract
stories.summary: Meta paused work with Sama last month after allegations about staff viewing private scenes filmed by smart glasses More than 1,000 low-paid workers in Kenya have been abruptly sacked by an outsourcing company contracted by Meta, in what activists said was a shocking move exposing the precariousness of tech jobs in the global south. Sama, a company based in Nairobi to which Meta outsourced content moderation and AI training work, announced on Thursday that the workers were being laid off after Me

stories.title: India fails to pass bill to boost women’s representation after delimitation row
stories.summary: Opposition accuses Narendra Modi government of using quotas as cover for redrawing electoral map The Indian government has failed to pass a bill to increase female representation in parliament after being accused of using the plan as a guise to redraw the country’s electoral map. It was the first time in 12 years in power that a constitutional amendment proposed by Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) government was not passed by parliament. Continue reading...

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, Russian strikes in Kyiv, Orthodox Easter and Karol G at Coachella – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Inside smoky shelters, a fast-paced, illegal card game has taken off in Solomon Islands
stories.summary: Growing numbers in the capital Honiara are playing the street card game Pass for a chance of a big payout, while risking big losses As the school day ends in Honiara, *Irene, a 43-year-old teacher in a floral dress with a yellow daisy in her bun, steps on to a minibus. After 10 minutes, Irene gets off the bus, walks down an alley, and enters a damp, smoky shelter. Plastic tables fill the space and playing cards are scattered on the floor. Irene has stopped by a hidden gambling table in a western

stories.title: Italy's Meloni breaks with Trump over war in Iran, pope
stories.summary: The war in Iran and a spat between two of the world's most influential Americans has forced Giorgia Meloni to choose sides, potentially pushing the Italian prime minister to align herself with Europe's center-right.

stories.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
stories.summary: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

stories.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
stories.summary: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

stories.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

stories.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
stories.summary: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?

stories.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
stories.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

stories.title: Venezuela and EU set out roadmap for renewed ties amid political thaw
stories.summary: Following talks in the Venezuelan parliament, Caracas and the EU have agreed on a new cooperation agenda. The shift aligns with a broader economic pivot, as the nation resumes ties with the IMF and the World Bank.

stories.title: 'I understand when you say no to war,' Brazil's Lula tells Spain's PM Pedro Sánchez
stories.summary: Lula and Sánchez are both outspoken in their criticism of US President Donald Trump, who has threatened both with punitive tariffs.

stories.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire: civilians return to ruined towns in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: A ceasefire in Lebanon has brought a pause in fighting after weeks of war. Residents are returning to damaged homes as questions remain over how long the truce will hold.

stories.title: White House vs the pope: What is behind the clash and Catholic just war doctrine?
stories.summary: Theologians Euronews spoke to believe that the escalating war of words between Washington and the Holy See has raised important questions over Catholic and Christian moral thought.

stories.title: First cruise ship transits the Strait of Hormuz since Iran war began
stories.summary: Iran and the US said the strategic waterway, which has been effectively closed since the beginning of the war, will be fully open to commercial traffic for the remainder of the ceasefire

stories.title: Iranian gunboats fire on tanker transiting the Strait of Hormuz, UK military says
stories.summary: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations Centre says two gunboats from Iran’s Revolutionary Guard opened fire on a tanker transiting the Strait of Hormuz after Iran said it had reimposed restrictions on the vital waterway.

stories.title: Israel accuses Hezbollah militants of violating ceasefire
stories.summary: A French UN peace worker has been killed in southern Lebanon as Israel accuses Hezbollah militants of breaching the 10-day ceasefire with Hezbollah.

stories.title: Starmer faces calls to step down after appointing Epstein associate
stories.summary: Opposition leaders demanded Starmer's resignation on Thursday after it emerged that US envoy Peter Mandelson was appointed despite having failed his security check.

stories.title: Sanctions don’t result in regime change. Whether against Iran or Russia, western countries need shrewder tactics | Simon Jenkins
stories.summary: Dissent cannot take hold in soil rendered barren by emigration and embargo. Political opposition needs academic and cultural exchange if it is to prosper The chancellor of the exchequer and the IMF agree . Britain’s economy is about to take its greatest hit for decades. This is collateral damage from the US’s war on Iran and the closing of the strait of Hormuz – and will be made worse by sanctioning Gulf oil exports . Britain has already been weakened by four years of sanctions against Russia ov

stories.title: Germany, the UK warn the Iran war distracts from Ukraine and oil price rises help Russia
stories.summary: Germany's defense minister warned that Russia "benefits from current developments in the Middle East," as the Ukraine Defense Contact Group met in Berlin. He said rising oil prices were filling Moscow's "war coffers."

stories.title: About 250 missing after boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsizes in Andaman Sea
stories.summary: Trawler set off from Bangladesh and reportedly capsized due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding About 250 people are missing after a boat carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals capsized in the Andaman Sea, according to the UN’s refugee and migration agencies. The agencies said the trawler carrying more than 250 men, women and children reportedly sank due to harsh weather and overcrowding. It had departed from Teknaf in southern Bangladesh and was bound for Malaysia. Continu

stories.title: More than half of Britons support rejoining EU 10 years on from Brexit vote
stories.summary: Experts say Labour’s ‘halfway house’ approach risks losing support from progressives and ‘red wall’ voters Support for rejoining the EU rather than simply rejoining the single market is growing among British voters, with more than 80% of Labour, Liberal Democrat and Green party supporters favouring this option, according to research mapping voter attitudes 10 years after the Brexit referendum. Labour’s “muted” approach to the issue means it risks losing support among progressive voters and in “r

stories.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
stories.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

stories.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
stories.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

stories.title: Military operation against Hezbollah 'still not complete,' Israel's defence minister says
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump announced a 10-day ceasefire on Friday after a meeting between the Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors, the first direct diplomatic talks between the two countries in decades.

stories.title: UK woman fears being stranded in Spain over new Home Office border rules
stories.summary: Natasha Cochrane de la Rosa was refused boarding on flight to London because she was not aware of the rule change A British woman has told how she fears being stranded in Spain for months after being refused boarding a on flight back home to London because she was not aware of new Home Office border rules. Natasha Cochrane de la Rosa, 26, was born in the UK to a British father and a Spanish mother, but because of archaic laws she was not entitled automatically to British citizenship because her
